step_1_context.text: In a compound consisting of noun + noun, often the second noun gives the general class of things to which the compound belongs and the first noun indicates the type within this class. The first noun u

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: In a compound consisting of noun + noun, often the second noun gives the general class of things to which the compound belongs and the first noun indicates the type within this class.
step_2_explanation.formula: Sometimes a noun + noun is not appropriate.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: Some compound nouns are made up of verbs and prepositions or adverbs, and may be related to a multi-word verb.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 43.1.1 He wants to be (a) a films star. Which word is incorrect?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: In noun+noun compounds, the first noun is usually singular (film star).

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 43.1.2 The tracks on his latest album range from (a) love songs to (b) pollution songs. Which phrase is unnatural?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: 'Pollution songs' doesn't refer to a well-known class of items, so noun+preposition+noun is preferred.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 43.2.1 Nathan tried to cover up the fact that he had lied. -> He attempted a ___ .
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: A compound noun from a phrasal verb is often hyphenated or joined.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 43.3.1 Since the attempt to assassinate him last year, the Defence Minister has been given ___ protection by the police.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: 'Round-the-clock' means all day and all night.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 43.3.2 Eleni has a refreshing, ___ approach to management. She's much less concerned with theory than with getting things done.
